category: Air Force PME / Enlisted Force Development
description: Enlisted force structure, EPR system, promotion testing, Air Force history, core values, dress and appearance (AFI 36-2903), customs and courtesies, Airman's Creed, AFI 36-2618.

questions.q: What are the three Air Force Core Values?
questions.a: Integrity First, Service Before Self, Excellence in All We Do.

questions.q: What is the first line of the Airman's Creed?
questions.a: I am an American Airman.

questions.q: The Airman's Creed states 'I am a _____, _____and _____.'
questions.a: Warrior. I have answered my nation's call.

questions.q: What AFI governs dress and personal appearance of Air Force personnel?
questions.a: AFI 36-2903, Dress and Personal Appearance of Air Force Personnel.

questions.q: What AFI is known as 'The Enlisted Force Structure'?
questions.a: AFI 36-2618, The Enlisted Force Structure (also called 'The Little Brown Book').

questions.q: What are the three tiers of the Air Force enlisted force?
questions.a: Junior Enlisted (E-1 to E-4), NCOs (E-5 to E-6), and Senior NCOs (E-7 to E-9).

questions.q: What rank is an Airman Basic (AB)?
What rank is an Airman (Amn)?
What rank is an Airman First Class (A1C)?
What rank is a Senior Airman (SrA)?
What rank is a Staff Sergeant (SSgt)?
What rank is a Technical Sergeant (TSgt)?
What rank is a Master Sergeant (MSgt)?
What rank is a Senior Master Sergeant (SMSgt)?
What rank is a Chief Master Sergeant (CMSgt)?
What is the highest enlisted rank in the Air Force?
questions.a: Chief Master Sergeant of the Air Force (CMSAF), a special E-9 position.

questions.q: What evaluation form is used for enlisted Airmen?
questions.a: The Enlisted Performance Report (EPR), AF Form 910 or 911.

questions.q: What does WAPS stand for?
questions.a: Weighted Airman Promotion System.

questions.q: What factors are included in WAPS scoring for promotion?
questions.a: Promotion Fitness Examination (PFE), Specialty Knowledge Test (SKT), EPR ratings, time-in-grade, time-in-service, decorations.

questions.q: What test do all enlisted Airmen take for promotion under WAPS?
questions.a: The Promotion Fitness Examination (PFE), which tests knowledge from the Air Force Handbook.

questions.q: To what rank does a Senior Airman typically get promoted based on time-in-service/time-in-grade (below-the-zone notwithstanding)?
questions.a: Staff Sergeant (E-5) — the first WAPS-tested promotion.

questions.q: When was the United States Air Force established as an independent service?
questions.a: September 18, 1947, under the National Security Act of 1947.

questions.q: What was the Air Force called before it became independent?
questions.a: The United States Army Air Forces (USAAF) / Army Air Corps.

questions.q: Who is considered the father of the United States Air Force?
questions.a: General Henry H. 'Hap' Arnold.

questions.q: Who was the first Chief of Staff of the Air Force?
questions.a: General Carl A. Spaatz.

questions.q: What was the name of the Army unit that flew the first military air mission, organized in 1907?
questions.a: The Aeronautical Division of the U.S. Army Signal Corps.

questions.q: What historic bomber raid did Lt Col Jimmy Doolittle lead in April 1942?
questions.a: The Doolittle Raid — B-25 bombers launched from USS Hornet to strike Tokyo.

questions.q: What Air Force operation airlifted supplies to West Berlin from 1948 to 1949?
questions.a: The Berlin Airlift (Operation Vittles).

questions.q: What is the proper way to address a Chief Master Sergeant?
questions.a: 'Chief' or 'Chief Master Sergeant.'

questions.q: When in uniform, how do enlisted Airmen greet a commissioned officer?
questions.a: By rendering a salute and offering a verbal greeting such as 'Good morning, Sir/Ma'am.'

questions.q: Are enlisted Airmen required to salute warrant officers?
questions.a: Yes. All commissioned and warrant officers are saluted by enlisted personnel.

questions.q: What is the proper response when an officer returns your salute?
questions.a: Drop the salute and continue on your way (or hold it if the officer has not yet returned it).

questions.q: Under AFI 36-2903, what are the two general categories of Air Force uniforms?
questions.a: Service uniforms (dress/semi-formal) and Utility uniforms (OCP/flight suit).

questions.q: What is the standard utility uniform of the Air Force?
questions.a: The Operational Camouflage Pattern (OCP) uniform.

questions.q: Under AFI 36-2903, are visible tattoos on the hands permitted?
questions.a: Yes, a single-band ring tattoo on one hand is permitted; full hand tattoos are not authorized (policy updated — check current AFI for latest guidance).

questions.q: What does PME stand for in the Air Force?
questions.a: Professional Military Education.

questions.q: What PME course do Senior Airmen attend (or complete via distance learning)?
questions.a: Airman Leadership School (ALS).

questions.q: What PME must a Technical Sergeant complete?
questions.a: NCO Academy (NCOA).

questions.q: What PME corresponds to the Senior NCO tier?
questions.a: Senior NCO Academy (SNCOA), required for Senior Master Sergeant.

questions.q: What is the Air Force's primary professional development guide for enlisted Airmen, often tested on the PFE?
questions.a: The Air Force Handbook 1 (AFH 1), Airman.

questions.q: What core value emphasizes that Airmen must not lie, steal, or cheat?
questions.a: Integrity First.

questions.q: What core value stresses professional duties take precedence over personal desires?
questions.a: Service Before Self.

questions.q: What core value requires continuous improvement and innovation?
questions.a: Excellence in All We Do.

questions.q: In the Airman's Creed, what phrase describes the commitment to defend?
questions.a: 'I will never leave an Airman behind, I will never falter, and I will not fail.'

questions.q: What is the Air Force term for the organizational flag carried by a unit?
questions.a: The guidon.

questions.q: What does 'Reveille' signify on an Air Force installation?
questions.a: The beginning of the official duty day and the raising of the flag.

questions.q: What does 'Retreat' signify on an Air Force installation?
questions.a: The end of the official duty day and the lowering of the flag.

questions.q: What should an Airman in uniform do outdoors when they hear the National Anthem or 'To the Colors'?
questions.a: Face the flag (or the music if the flag is not visible), stand at attention, and render a salute.

questions.q: According to AFI 36-2618, what is the primary duty of all NCOs?
questions.a: To lead and manage teams, develop subordinate Airmen, and support the mission.

questions.q: What force management tool replaced 'Up or Out' for enlisted promotions?
questions.a: The Selective Retention process (High Year of Tenure / HYT limits determine maximum years at each grade).

questions.q: What is the maximum number of years a Staff Sergeant (E-5) can serve under HYT?
questions.a: 20 years (as of current policy — verify against latest AFI updates).

questions.q: What year did the Air Force transition from the Airman Battle Uniform (ABU) to the OCP?
questions.a: Full transition was completed by April 1, 2021.
